nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Friston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Freestone  .

variants.name: Freestone
meanings: English: habitational name from Friston or Freston (both Suffolk), Frieston (Lincolnshire), or possibly Friston (Sussex).

nameMeanings.name: Know
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Kney, unexplained (see Kanoy ).

variants.name: Kanoy
meanings: Americanized form of German Kney, unexplained. The surname Kney is virtually non-existing in Germany; it is (also) found in France (Alsace), but it is very rare there. Compare Canoy 1, Cannoy , and Knoy .

History: The progenitor of the American Kanoys was Hans Bernhard Knoy, born in 1718 in Bavaria, Germany. His father's surname was Kney, while his American descendants also bear the surnames Canoy and Cannoy.

nameMeanings.name: As
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Dieter, Gunter, Hans, Jurgen, Manfred, Trud.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German ast, German Ast ‘knot (in wood), branch’, hence a nickname for a tough or awkward individual or a metonymic occupational name for a lumberjack. As a Jewish name it is artificial.
North German: occupational name, from Middle Low German arste ‘physician’, a variant of Arzt .

variants.name: Arzt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Aron, Isidor, Itzchak, Noam, Sholom.  German Elke, Franz, Alois, Hans.
North German: variant of   Artz  .
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a physician, German Arzt, from Middle High German arzet ‘physician’.

nameMeanings.name: Haget
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: diminutive of   Page  .
English: variant of   Padgett  .

variants.name: Paggett
meanings: English: variant of   Paget  .

variants.name: Pagett
meanings: English: variant of   Paget   or   Padgett  .

variants.name: Page
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

en.lifeSummary: When Alice De Friston Know As Haget was born about 1180, her father, William de Friston, was 20 and her mother, Alice Haget, was 29. She had at least 2 daughters with Jordan de St Mary. She died about 3 February 1247, at the age of 68.
